verdict_reasoning: BAX is a cytosolic/mitochondrial-outer-membrane pro-apoptotic BCL2-family effector. During apoptosis it oligomerizes and permeabilizes the MOM, and externalized/surface-exposed BAX has been documented on apoptotic/dying cells as part of cell-death-associated outer-leaflet display. This places it in cell-state-induced surface accessibility (apoptosis/ICD context), not baseline surface residency. No TM anchor at baseline PM, no GPI, no stable TM-partner tethering; no cancer ecto-pool dominance, no germline-restricted surface, not a lysosomal TM protein, no documented constitutive PM cycling pool, no wash-resistant covalent tethering to a surface partner.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether documented apoptosis-associated BAX surface exposure on intact dying cells is robust enough to credit as contextual vs. strictly MOM-internal.
